title: Volume of distribution
body_html:
The volume of distribution, VD = theoretical volume that the total amount of administered drug would have to occupy to provide the same concentration as it currently is in blood plasma
  • Low VD drugs are confined to plasma
  • Medium VD drugs are distributed in extracellular space
  • High VD drugs are distributed in the tissues

The VD of a drug is dependent on a number of factors:

Increases VDDecreases VD
Liver failure
Renal failure
Pregnancy
Dehydration
Large molecules (e.g. heparin, as they are confined to plasma)
High plasma protein
Hydrophillicity
High charge

Examples of high VD drugsExamples of low VD drugs
Tricyclic antidepressants
Morphine
Digoxin
Phenytoin
Chloroquine
Salicylates
Heparin
Insulin
Warfarin
3
300
